metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ALL four `_live_jira_ready()`-gated modules (`test_link_sync_live.py`, `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`, `test_reconcile_live.py`, `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py`) have `@pytest.mark.jira_live` applied, and update the Scope section to list all four.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that only two modules are 'gated on `_live_jira_ready()`' — `test_link_sync_live.py` and `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py` — and scopes the `jira_live` marker application and the Scope section to those two. A grep for `_live_jira_ready` reveals FOUR modules use this gate: also `test_reconcile_live.py` (line 35) and `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py` (line 45). Omitting these two from the marker means their tests silently skip without triggering the canary, leaving Jira API/SDK drift in those modules undetected — the exact gap the plan says it closes.
findings.location: What section item 2 ('apply @pytest.mark.jira_live to the two Jira-credential-gated live modules'); Scope section (lists only test_link_sync_live.py and test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py for marker application)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section and What item 2, replace 'the two Jira-credential-gated live modules' with all four: `test_link_sync_live.py`, `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`, `test_reconcile_live.py`, and `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `test_reconcile_live.py` and `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py` (which use the same `_live_jira_ready()` guard) should also receive `@pytest.mark.jira_live` and be covered by the canary, or explicitly document the rationale for excluding them from the marker scope.
findings.finding: The `jira_live` marker is scoped only to `test_link_sync_live.py` and `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`, but two additional external modules — `test_reconcile_live.py` and `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py` — use the identical `_live_jira_ready()` guard and will also silently skip when Jira creds/acli are absent. The canary as specified does not detect all-skip in those modules, leaving Jira API/reconciler drift and verified-fake contract drift unguarded.
findings.location: ## What, item 2 ('Register a `jira_live` marker … apply `@pytest.mark.jira_live` to the two Jira-credential-gated live modules')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `JIRA_PROJECT: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT }}` to the `external` job's env block (mirroring reconcile-bridge.yml line 211) so live mutation tests target the operator-provisioned disposable project, not the `'DIG'` default.
findings.finding: The workflow step additions in the plan do not map the `JIRA_PROJECT` environment variable into the `external` job. Both `test_link_sync_live.py` and `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py` (and `test_reconcile_live.py`) call `_build_client()` which falls back to `os.environ.get('JIRA_PROJECT', 'DIG')` — meaning issues will be created/reconciled in project key `DIG` rather than the operator-provisioned dedicated disposable test project, unless that project's key happens to be `DIG`.
findings.location: ## What, item 1 (Workflow env additions) and ## Scope ('.github/workflows/external-integration.yml')
findings.suggested_fix: In the `Run the external-integration suite` step's env block, add `JIRA_PROJECT: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT }}` alongside the other JIRA_* vars. Update AC1 to include this mapping.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `jira_live` marker application to all four modules gated on `_live_jira_ready()`: also apply `@pytest.mark.jira_live` to `tests/external/test_reconcile_live.py` and `tests/external/test_verified_fake_contract_live.py`, and update the §Scope list accordingly.
findings.finding: The `jira_live` marker scope vocabulary is under-enumerated: the plan identifies 'the two Jira-credential-gated live modules — test_link_sync_live.py and test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py' but the codebase contains four modules gated on `_live_jira_ready()`. The two excluded modules (test_reconcile_live.py and test_verified_fake_contract_live.py) also skip when Jira creds/acli are missing; without the `jira_live` marker, the canary will never fire for them even when they all skip.
findings.location: §What item 2 — 'apply @pytest.mark.jira_live to the two Jira-credential-gated live modules'; §Scope — 'tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py, tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py'
findings.suggested_fix: In §Scope, replace 'tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py, tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py' with all four modules: add 'tests/external/test_reconcile_live.py, tests/external/test_verified_fake_contract_live.py'. Apply `@pytest.mark.jira_live` (or `pytestmark = [pytest.mark.external, pytest.mark.jira_live]`) at module level in all four files.

findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section asserts the live test 'deletes every artifact it created (links then issues).' The actual cleanup code in `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py` lines 418-431 does NOT call `delete_issue_link` — the code comment explicitly states the command 'currently hangs (no subprocess timeout — bug d843)' and deletes only the issues (which also removes links as a side effect). The plan's description 'links then issues' misrepresents the cleanup order and mechanism.
findings.location: Why § — live test cleanup description
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'deletes every artifact it created (links then issues)' with 'deletes each created issue via delete_issue (which cascades link removal), skipping delete_issue_link due to a known hang (bug d843).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a single, named pytest hook mechanism (e.g. `pytest_runtest_logreport` to collect `jira_live` reports + `pytest_sessionfinish` to assert; OR a session-scoped autouse fixture with `yield`; remove the `pytest_terminal_summary` slash-or option) and ensure the behavioral test's 'constructed report objects' description matches the chosen hook's actual signature.
findings.finding: The plan uses slash notation `pytest_terminal_summary / session hook` without committing to a specific pytest hook, then instructs the behavioral test to 'drive the hook function directly with constructed report objects' — but `pytest_terminal_summary` receives a `TerminalReporter` (not individual report objects), while individual report objects are passed to `pytest_runtest_logreport`. These two mechanisms require different function signatures and test harnesses. An executor cannot determine which hook to implement without guessing, and a wrong choice (e.g. implementing `pytest_terminal_summary` but writing tests that pass `TestReport` objects directly) could yield a behavioral test that passes while the production hook never fires.
findings.location: What §2, 'conftest.py — the marker-scoped fail-on-all-skip session hook'; What §3 behavioral test description; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'add a `pytest_terminal_summary` / session hook' with: 'add two conftest hooks: (1) `pytest_runtest_logreport(report)` — appends each report whose `nodeid` maps to a `jira_live`-marked item to a session-level list; (2) `pytest_sessionfinish(session, exitstatus)` — reads the list and calls `session.exitstatus = 1` (or sets a flag read by a `pytest_configure`-registered plugin) if all collected `jira_live` reports are skips and `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is set.' Then update the behavioral test description to 'construct `SimpleNamespace` objects mimicking `TestReport` with `.nodeid`, `.when`, `.outcome` attributes and pass them to the `pytest_runtest_logreport` hook, then call `pytest_sessionfinish` with a mock session object and assert `session.exitstatus`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact pytest hook primitive(s) the fail-on-all-skip canary uses (e.g., `pytest_runtest_logreport` to accumulate per-report + `pytest_sessionfinish`/`pytest_terminal_summary` to check and signal, or solely `pytest_sessionfinish` walking `session.testscollected`), and state where the jira_live collected/executed counters are stored and when they are reset, so the behavioral test's 'constructed report objects' instruction is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The fail-on-all-skip hook's counting protocol and state-accumulation placement are never specified. The plan refers to 'pytest_terminal_summary / session hook' (ambiguous slash) and instructs the behavioral test to 'drive the hook function directly with constructed report objects', which is only coherent for pytest_runtest_logreport (one report at a time, needing a module-level accumulator), not for pytest_terminal_summary (receives a TerminalReporter object, no per-report construction) or pytest_sessionfinish (receives Session + exitstatus). The implementer cannot determine where the jira_live collected/executed counters live, when they are incremented, and which hook signals failure.
findings.location: §What item 2 — 'conftest hook that, when REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL is truthy, REPORTS the jira_live executed-vs-skipped counts'; AC2 — 'behavioral test driving the hook over constructed report objects'
findings.suggested_fix: Commit to a two-hook design: `pytest_runtest_logreport(report)` appends to a `_jira_live_reports: list[pytest.TestReport]` module-level list in `tests/external/conftest.py` when the report's nodeid's test item carries the `jira_live` marker; then `pytest_sessionfinish(session, exitstatus)` reads that list, counts collected (those with `when=='call'`) vs. non-skipped, and sets `session.exitstatus = pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED` when all skipped. The behavioral test then constructs fake `pytest.TestReport` objects and calls the two hook functions directly.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion: confirm whether `pytest_terminal_summary` can force a nonzero session exit (it cannot in standard pytest — `exitstatus` is passed by value), and replace the hook specification with the correct mechanism — `pytest_sessionfinish` (mutate `session.exitstatus`) or `pytest_runtest_logreport` + `pytest_sessionfinish` combination — updating the AC and behavioral test spec accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the conftest hook 'FAILS the session (nonzero exit) iff ≥1 jira_live test was COLLECTED but ZERO jira_live tests EXECUTED' and names it a 'pytest_terminal_summary / session hook'. However, pytest_terminal_summary is an informational reporting hook invoked after the exit code is already finalized — it cannot directly cause a nonzero process exit. The established pytest pattern for overriding session exit status is pytest_sessionfinish (which exposes session.exitstatus for mutation) or raising pytest.exit() inside pytest_collection_finish / pytest_runtest_protocol. This is an unverified capability assertion: the named hook cannot deliver the specified behavior.
findings.location: What § 2 / tests/external/conftest.py
findings.suggested_fix: Use two hooks: (1) `pytest_runtest_logreport(report)` in conftest.py to accumulate per-marker collected/executed counts into a session-scoped store (e.g., `config._jira_live_stats`), and (2) `pytest_sessionfinish(session, exitstatus)` to read those counts and call `session.exitstatus = pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED` (or `os._exit(1)`) when REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL is set and all jira_live tests skipped. The behavioral test then drives both hooks over constructed TestReport objects.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the hook definition whether `XFAIL`/`XPASS` pytest outcomes count as 'executed' (recommended: yes — any outcome other than `skipped` means the test ran against live Jira, so xfail should count as executed).
findings.finding: The plan specifies the canary fires when zero `jira_live` tests 'EXECUTED (passed or failed)'. `test_link_sync_live.py::test_link_primitives_roundtrip_live` carries `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)` (confirmed at line 71-78). An xfail outcome (`XFAIL`) is neither a 'pass' nor a 'fail' in pytest's report terminology — it has outcome `'xfailed'`. The plan never specifies whether XFAIL counts as 'executed' in the hook's counter, leaving an implementer uncertain whether a test that always XFAILs (e.g. because the acli link bug is live) satisfies the 'at least one executed' threshold or falls into the silent-skip bucket.
findings.location: What section item 2 (conftest hook definition: 'EXECUTED (passed or failed)'); tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py lines 71-78
findings.suggested_fix: Amend What item 2 to read: 'EXECUTED (outcome is passed, failed, xfailed, or xpassed — any outcome other than skipped)' to unambiguously include xfail outcomes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the conftest hook spec that 'EXECUTED' includes `xfailed`/`xpassed` outcomes (i.e. count a test as executed if its `pytest_terminal_summary` stats entry appears in any of `passed`, `failed`, `xfailed`, `xpassed`), and update the behavioral test spec accordingly.
findings.finding: `test_link_sync_live.py::test_link_primitives_roundtrip_live` is decorated with BOTH `@_skip` (a `pytest.mark.skipif` that gates on Jira creds+acli) AND `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)`. When Jira creds ARE present (the intended schedule-run scenario), this test executes and produces an `xfailed` or `xpassed` outcome — neither of which is 'passed' or 'failed' in pytest's terminology. The plan's canary hook fires 'iff ≥1 jira_live test was COLLECTED but ZERO jira_live tests EXECUTED (passed or failed)'. An xfailed outcome is not 'passed or failed', so the canary would incorrectly fire even when this test ran against live Jira and produced an xfail result — defeating the canary's purpose for this specific test.
findings.location: ## What / item 2 — Fail-on-all-skip Jira canary; tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py lines 70-79
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook implementation, define 'executed' as: `stats.get('passed', []) + stats.get('failed', []) + stats.get('xfailed', []) + stats.get('xpassed', [])` for jira_live-marked items, not just passed+failed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `jira_live` marker should be applied to `test_link_sync_live.py::test_link_primitives_roundtrip_live` given its `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)` decoration, and update the hook's 'executed' definition to count XFAIL/XPASS outcomes as 'executed' (not skipped) if the marker is applied.
findings.finding: The `jira_live` all-skip hook is specified to fire when 'zero jira_live tests EXECUTED (passed or failed)'. However, `test_link_sync_live.py::test_link_primitives_roundtrip_live` carries `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)` and its docstring notes the link primitives are broken against the live ACLI. If this test is marked `jira_live` and actually runs (creds+acli present), pytest records its outcome as XFAIL — a distinct outcome that is neither 'passed' nor 'failed'. The hook as described would count XFAIL as 'zero executed', triggering a false canary failure even when the test ran with live Jira. This is a capability gap in the hook's 'executed' definition.
findings.location: What §2 (fail-on-all-skip hook) + tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py line 71-78
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook, count a test as 'executed' if its outcome is in {passed, failed, xfailed, xpassed} — i.e., any outcome that is not 'skipped'. In pytest_runtest_logreport, check `report.when == 'call' and report.outcome != 'skipped'`. Alternatively, explicitly exclude the xfail test from the jira_live marker and note the exclusion in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the hook's decision rule to explicitly classify `xfail`/`xpass` outcomes: state whether an xfailed test counts as 'executed' (and add an xfail report to the behavioral test's scenarios), to prevent false canary triggers on the documented `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)` test in `test_link_sync_live.py`.
findings.finding: The hook's decision rule 'FAILS iff ≥1 jira_live test was COLLECTED but ZERO jira_live tests EXECUTED (passed or failed)' never defines whether an `xfail` outcome counts as 'executed'. test_link_sync_live.py's sole test is decorated `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False)`, meaning when Jira creds are present and the test runs but the acli call fails as documented, pytest records it as `xfail` — which is neither a pass nor a strict failure. The processing rule is not co-located with the classification of executed outcomes.
findings.location: §What item 2 — 'FAILS iff ≥1 jira_live test was COLLECTED but ZERO jira_live tests EXECUTED (passed or failed)'; tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py:71-78
findings.suggested_fix: Update the hook decision rule to: 'FAILS iff ≥1 jira_live test was COLLECTED but ZERO jira_live tests had outcome in {passed, failed, xfailed, xpassed}' (i.e., any non-skip outcome counts as executed). Add a fourth behavioral test scenario: a session where the only jira_live test reports xfail does NOT fire the canary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `pytest_terminal_summary` hook wraps its body in a try/except, logs a clear error and re-raises (or calls `pytest.exit`) on unexpected failure, so hook errors are surfaced as actionable messages rather than opaque internal tracebacks.
findings.finding: The conftest `pytest_terminal_summary` hook has no specified error handling: if it throws an unhandled exception (e.g., unexpected structure of the terminal reporter object, missing attribute), pytest will propagate an internal error rather than a clean, actionable FAILED message, potentially masking the real skip-count signal or confusing the operator.
findings.location: tests/external/conftest.py — 'pytest_terminal_summary / session hook' (What section, item 2)
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook implementation, wrap the skip-count logic in a try/except Exception block: on unexpected error, print a clear diagnostic (e.g., `print('[canary] hook error — could not determine jira_live skip status: {e}')`) and re-raise so the session still fails loudly rather than silently passing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fourth behavioral test scenario for the hook: zero `jira_live` tests collected (with `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1`) should either raise an error or be documented as a deliberate no-op with a rationale — and add a config-as-artifact assertion that `@pytest.mark.jira_live` is applied in both `test_link_sync_live.py` and `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`.
findings.finding: The behavioral test plan covers three hook scenarios (all-skip fires, ≥1 executed passes, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL unset = no-op) but omits the zero-collected case: if zero jira_live tests are collected (e.g., markers accidentally not applied or stripped), the hook's guard condition '≥1 collected but zero executed' is never true, the canary silently does not fire, and the exact drift this plan is designed to prevent goes undetected. This zero-collected bypass is the primary silent-failure mode of the new canary itself and is not tested.
findings.location: Tests section / 'a behavioral test of the fail-on-all-skip hook over SIMULATED session reports' + AC line 2 ('PASSES when a jira_live test executed')
findings.suggested_fix: In the behavioral test module, add: `def test_hook_noop_when_zero_collected(...)`: construct a session with REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1 but zero jira_live items collected; assert the hook either raises a clear error (preferred — zero collected likely means marker misconfiguration) or at minimum documents this is accepted behavior. In the config-as-artifact test, add a grep/AST assertion that `pytest.mark.jira_live` appears in both live test files, making marker-removal a CI failure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the existing `live` marker (already registered in pyproject.toml with identical Jira/acli/dedicated-project/cleanup semantics) can serve as the canary scope instead of adding a new `jira_live` marker — or document a concrete semantic distinction that justifies both.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `jira_live` pytest marker whose semantics — Jira credentials required, dedicated test project, acli binary, bilateral cleanup — are substantively identical to the already-registered `live` marker in pyproject.toml (`"live: live Jira reconciler-validation tests (story d01e); hit a dedicated test project + require JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN + acli; excluded from the default run, self-skip without live env; GUARANTEE bilateral cleanup"`). Adding `jira_live` creates a second marker token for the same concept, proliferating the marker config surface.
findings.location: What section, item 2 ('Register a `jira_live` marker') and pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options].markers
findings.suggested_fix: Reuse `@pytest.mark.live` for the conftest hook scope (key the hook on the `live` marker, which already covers these exact tests). If the distinction is that `live` is broader than 'Jira live' (e.g. it could include non-Jira live tests), narrow it in the plan with a concrete example; otherwise drop `jira_live` and extend the existing `live` marker.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that `on.schedule` triggers ALL jobs in `external-integration.yml` including `langfuse-trace`; either accept this (document it) or restructure the workflow so only the `external` job runs on schedule (e.g. via a `if: github.event_name == 'schedule'` condition on `langfuse-trace` or splitting into a separate workflow).
findings.finding: The `on.schedule` trigger added to `external-integration.yml` applies at the workflow level, meaning both the `external` job AND the `langfuse-trace` job (which spins up a full Docker Compose Langfuse stack, waits up to 5 minutes, and tears it down) would run every Monday 06:00 UTC. The plan only acknowledges the `external` job receiving Jira env vars and acli, but never mentions that the langfuse-trace job will also be scheduled weekly — a potentially expensive/unintended side effect that may also fail if no LLM key is set on the schedule trigger.
findings.location: ## What / item 1 — Workflow (external-integration.yml): add an on.schedule trigger
findings.suggested_fix: Add `if: github.event_name != 'schedule'` to the `langfuse-trace` job definition, or document in the plan that the langfuse-trace job running weekly is intentional and LLM keys are guaranteed on schedule runs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the existing `live` marker (pyproject.toml line 433) and explain why a new `jira_live` marker is preferred over reusing `live` (e.g. `live` is also used in `tests/integration/` and the canary hook must be scoped only to `tests/external/` Jira tests).
findings.finding: A `live` marker is already registered in `pyproject.toml` (line 433: `"live: live Jira reconciler-validation tests (story d01e); hit a dedicated test project + require JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN + acli; excluded from the default run, self-skip without live env; GUARANTEE bilateral cleanup"`) and is used by `tests/integration/test_reconcile_live_e2e.py`. The plan introduces a second, semantically overlapping `jira_live` marker for the external tier without acknowledging the existing `live` marker or explaining why it cannot be reused. This risks confusion and marker proliferation.
findings.location: ## What / item 2 — 'Register a jira_live marker (pyproject [tool.pytest.ini_options].markers)'; pyproject.toml line 433

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why a new `jira_live` marker is needed instead of reusing/extending the existing `live` marker already registered in `pyproject.toml` (defined as 'live Jira reconciler-validation tests… require JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN + acli'); if they are semantically distinct, document the distinction explicitly.
findings.finding: The repo already registers a 'live' pytest marker in pyproject.toml defined as 'live Jira reconciler-validation tests (story d01e); hit a dedicated test project + require JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN + acli; excluded from the default run, self-skip without live env; GUARANTEE bilateral cleanup' — which is semantically identical to the plan's proposed new 'jira_live' marker. The plan introduces a second, overlapping marker without acknowledging the existing one or justifying the divergence.
findings.location: What § 2 / pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options].markers
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) reuse the existing `live` marker — apply `@pytest.mark.live` to the two Jira-gated modules and scope the conftest hook to `live`-marked tests — or (b) keep `jira_live` but update its pyproject description to clearly differentiate it from `live` and explain in the plan why the narrower scope is deliberate (e.g., `live` also covers reconcile_live.py tests which have a different gating mechanism).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a least-privilege note to AC §3: specify that `JIRA_API_TOKEN` should be a Jira user/service-account credential scoped to only the dedicated disposable test project (project-level Create/Edit/Delete permissions; no org-admin or cross-project grants).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that the `JIRA_API_TOKEN` used in CI should be scoped/restricted to the dedicated disposable Jira test project only. The live tests perform real write mutations (`create_issue`, `set_relationship`) and the plan mandates a dedicated test project for blast-radius isolation — but if the token carries org-wide or project-admin permissions (a common default when generating Atlassian API tokens), a misconfigured test or accidental project misconfiguration could mutate production Jira data.
findings.location: What §1 (acli auth step) and Acceptance Criteria §3 (operator-attested)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the operator-attested AC to read: '…provisions a DEDICATED disposable Jira test project AND configures the JIRA_API_TOKEN as a service-account token with permissions limited to that project only (Create Issues, Edit Issues, Delete Issues on the test project; no site-admin or cross-project grants).'

findings.finding: The plan describes the acli download-verify-install step as 'sha256-checked' and instructs mirroring reconcile-bridge.yml's step 'verbatim in shape' — but reconcile-bridge.yml's actual SHA256 check is a soft warning (not a hard failure) when `ACLI_SHA256` is unset. If an operator configures `ACLI_VERSION` but omits `ACLI_SHA256` (a realistic error given AC item 3 lists them as a pair with no enforcement), the scheduled workflow downloads and executes an unverified binary from an external CDN on a runner that simultaneously holds `JIRA_API_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, and `OPENAI_API_KEY`. The plan's 'sha256-checked' characterization misrepresents the actual control: integrity verification is optional/advisory, not enforced.
findings.location: What §1 ('the Download + verify + install acli step (pinned vars.ACLI_VERSION + vars.ACLI_SHA256, sha256-checked)') / reconcile-bridge.yml lines 156–159
findings.suggested_fix: In What §1, change the instruction for the Download+verify step to: 'if ACLI_SHA256 is unset or empty, the step must exit 1 (not merely warn) — the scheduled canary run is a security-sensitive context because the runner holds multiple live API secrets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief entry to CLAUDE.md (or an ADR) documenting the `jira_live`-marker / fail-on-all-skip canary pattern, its scope (marker-gated, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL-guarded), and the convention for extending it to future external tiers.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel test-infrastructure pattern (fail-on-all-skip canary + jira_live marker convention) that future contributors may want to extend to other external tiers, but no documentation update (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc entry) is planned to capture the pattern's intent, scope, and extension contract.
findings.location: ## What — item 2 (conftest hook) and ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to CLAUDE.md under a 'Testing Conventions' or 'External Integration Tests' heading: describe (1) the jira_live marker's purpose, (2) the conftest hook's trigger condition, and (3) the convention that new Jira-gated live tests must carry the marker. Keep it to ~5 lines.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the live test's finally-block cleanup is best-effort (suppress exceptions from delete calls) and document whether retry/backoff (or a note that transient failures are acceptable noise for a weekly canary) is in scope, so cleanup failures don't mask the original test failure.
findings.finding: No retry or timeout is specified for the scheduled workflow's Jira API calls (create_issue, set_relationship, delete). A transient Jira API outage or rate-limit during the weekly run will cause a test failure AND may leave orphaned Jira issues if the finally-block's delete calls also fail under the same outage.
findings.location: What section item 1 (workflow) and Scope — test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py
findings.suggested_fix: In the finally block, wrap each delete call in its own try/except and log (not re-raise) any deletion failure, so cleanup errors are observable but do not shadow the primary test result. Add a brief note in the plan that transient API failures producing red weekly runs are acceptable noise (no retry needed) OR specify a simple retry decorator on the API calls.

findings.finding: The plan's skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) provably fails to match the actual skip messages used by LLM-credential-gated external tests. Reading every `_skip` definition across `tests/external/`: LLM tests use `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"`, `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"`, and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` — none of which contain `cred`, `acli`, `live`, or `api.token`. The keyword `api.token` matches zero skip messages in the entire repo. So the hook's condition (a) would never fire for an all-LLM-skip scenario, making the canary inoperative for the LLM tier.
findings.location: What § item 2 — fail-on-all-skip hook; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add keywords `anthropic`, `openai`, `api_key`, `agents` to the matching vocabulary, OR (more robustly) have each live test apply a custom pytest marker (e.g. `@pytest.mark.live_prerequisite`) and key the hook off that marker rather than skip-reason text.

findings.finding: The canary hook's skip-reason keyword set (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) does not match the actual skip reason strings used by LLM-gated external tests. Specifically, 'no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra' (used by test_workflow_live, test_review_kernel_rules_live, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live), 'needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' (test_execution_mode_live), and 'needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY' (test_llm_trace) contain none of the four patterns. If LLM credentials are absent but Jira credentials ARE present, LLM-gated tests skip with unrecognized reasons — the hook may not count them as 'credential-gated skips', so condition (a) may fail to fire for an all-skip LLM tier, defeating the canary for that class of tests.
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest hook) — 'reason matching, case-insensitively, `cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`'
findings.suggested_fix: Match on any of: `cred`, `acli`, `live`, `api.token`, `api_key`, `anthropic`, `openai`, `agents extra`, `langfuse` (case-insensitive). Alternatively, invert the approach: mark always-on tests with a custom marker (e.g. `pytest.mark.always_on`) and treat ALL other skips in the external tier as credential-gated, which is more robust to future skip reason wording changes.

findings.finding: The plan's skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) is asserted to capture 'missing live prerequisite' skips, but is unverified against the actual skip messages used in tests/external/. Three LLM-gated tests use skip reasons that match NONE of these keywords: `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` (test_workflow_live.py:41, test_review_kernel_rules_live.py:48, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py:45), `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` (test_execution_mode_live.py:25), and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` (test_llm_trace.py:80–83). In a scheduled run where LLM keys are absent but Jira creds are present, these tests skip with unmatched reasons, so condition (a) of the hook's failure logic never fires for the LLM tier — LLM-tier all-skip goes undetected.
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest fail-on-all-skip hook) / AC line 2 — skip-reason keyword vocabulary
findings.suggested_fix: Add `key` and `anthropic` (or `openai`) to the matching vocabulary so `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` and `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` are classified as missing-live-prerequisite skips. Alternatively, define the match as 'any skip reason that does NOT contain "inert by default"' (i.e. treat only the explicit conftest opt-in skip as non-credential-gated), which covers all current and future credential-skip patterns without a keyword list.

findings.finding: The skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) defined for condition (a) of the fail-on-all-skip hook provably fails to match multiple real live-prerequisite skip messages used in the codebase: `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` (test_workflow_live.py, test_review_kernel_rules_live.py, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py), `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` (test_execution_mode_live.py), and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` (test_llm_trace.py) contain none of the four tokens case-insensitively. This means real credential-gated skips will go undetected by condition (a), defeating the canary's detection logic for the LLM-tier tests.
findings.location: What, item 2 — conftest hook condition (a); tests/external/test_workflow_live.py:41; tests/external/test_execution_mode_live.py:25; tests/external/test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py:45; tests/external/test_llm_trace.py:80
findings.suggested_fix: Instead of a keyword allowlist for condition (a), invert the logic: treat any skip reason that is NOT the fixed opt-in guard message (`'external tests are inert by default'`) as a credential-gated skip. This is more robust and requires no vocabulary maintenance as new live tests are added.

findings.finding: The plan asserts `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests` 'needs no creds and never skips' and uses it as the canonical example of an always-on guard test whose execution cannot mask an all-skip live tier. In reality, the test body calls `pytest.importorskip("pydantic_ai.models")` at line 19, which issues a SKIP (not a failure) when the `[agents]` extra is absent (lean lane). The plan's exclusion logic rests on this test being unconditionally executed, but it can itself skip.
findings.location: What § item 2 — always-on guard test exclusion; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Use `test_deps_live.py::test_live_real_packages_refute` (no credentials, no importorskip, no skipif) as the canonical always-on example, or define the 'always-on' category by the absence of a credential-skip marker rather than by a named test.

findings.finding: The exclusion rule 'always-on guard tests that carry NO credential skip are excluded from the executed-live count' is a decision rule stated in the hook description but not given a mechanical specification that can be co-located with the counter logic. The only example is `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests`, but other tests also have no Jira credential requirement (e.g., `test_deps_live.py` needs only network, `test_execution_mode_live.py` has its own `@pytest.mark.skipif(not os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'))` which IS credential-gated). There is no pytest marker, naming convention, or enumerated list that lets the hook distinguish 'always-on guard' from 'credential-gated' at runtime.
findings.location: What, item 2 — 'always-on guard tests excluded from the executed count'; AC item 2
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a `pytest.mark.external_guard` marker applied to tests like `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests` that never skip on credentials; the hook excludes tests carrying this marker from the 'credential-gated executed' count.

findings.finding: The conftest hook must exclude 'always-on guard' tests from the executed-live count (so they can't mask a real all-skip), but the plan never specifies the MECHANISM by which a passing test is classified as an always-on guard vs a credential-gated live test. For skipped tests the skip-reason regex (cred/acli/live/api.token) provides classification, but a passing test has no skip reason. An executor must either hard-code a list of guard test IDs, introduce a pytest mark, or use a naming convention — none of which is specified.
findings.location: What §2 (fail-on-all-skip canary) / Acceptance Criteria §2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to §2: 'Tests are classified as credential-gated by the presence of `@pytest.mark.live_credential_required`; the hook counts only marked tests in the executed-live tally and only considers skip-reason matches on marked tests for condition (a). Always-on guards carry no such mark and are invisible to both conditions.'

findings.finding: The 'always-on guard tests EXCLUDED from the executed-live count' decision rule is stated as a processing protocol in prose but no co-located mechanism is specified: the plan names no pytest marker, no nodeid pattern, no hook primitive, and no classification attribute that the hook implementation can use to distinguish always-on guard tests (like `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests`) from credential-gated live tests. The counter accumulation hook primitive (e.g. `pytest_runtest_logreport`, module-level global, plugin attribute) is also unspecified.
findings.location: What item 2: 'always-on guard tests that carry NO credential skip … are EXCLUDED from the executed-live count, so their unconditional execution cannot mask an all-skip live tier'

findings.finding: The conftest hook must distinguish 'always-on guard tests' (excluded from the executed-live count) from 'credential-gated live tests' (included), but the plan provides only one example test name and no general classification rule — leaving the executor to choose an appropriate mechanism (e.g., a pytest marker, a naming convention, or skip-reason-based inference).
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest hook) and AC bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What § 2 spec: 'A test counts as a credential-gated live test iff its collected skip-reason (if any) matches the `cred|acli|live|api\.token` pattern; tests that EXECUTE without ever producing a matching skip-reason are treated as always-on guards and excluded from the executed-live count. No marker or naming convention is needed — the distinction is derived entirely from the skip-reason signal already being tracked for condition (a).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
